D3 Chemistry L3 Apply Reference

Lightfastness Ratings — ASTM I to V

ASTM D4302 classes a pigment I (excellent, lasts >100 y) through V (fugitive, gone in <2 y); the label on the paint tube tells you the expected life

ASTM D4302 (artist oil paint) and D5398 (acrylic) classify pigments by accelerated-aging fading: I excellent (>100 y of museum display), II very good (50–100 y), III fair, IV poor, V fugitive. Manufacturers print a Roman numeral on the tube — the difference between “cadmium red ASTM I” and “Indian yellow ASTM V” is centuries of art.

Pick the ASTM class to see typical pigments at that rating, the expected lifetime under museum lighting, and a sample fade comparison after 50 years.
